skill.name: maintenance
skill.description: Organizes project maintenance files by checking Plans.md, session logs, and CLAUDE.md for cleanup or archive needs.
skill.summary: Reviews project planning and memory files, then recommends cleanup or archive actions.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution, filesystem, weak-crypto, and entropy findings. Review found no malicious payload, network access, credential handling, or prompt-injection text, but the skill intentionally allows Bash and file editing for project cleanup, so it should publish with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled File Maintenance Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash as an allowed tool and instructs the agent to read auto-cleanup guidance before acting. The referenced guidance includes shell commands that inspect project files and create archive directories. This is legitimate for cleanup work, but it can alter local project state if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and shell examples are explicit, and the cleanup purpose is consistent across both files. Confidence is not higher because the commands are documented examples rather than packaged executable scripts.

medium_findings.title: Project File Modification and Archiving
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to move old tasks, split session logs, create archive directories, and make backups. These actions are expected for maintenance, but accidental data movement or deletion could occur if the agent applies the guidance too broadly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file maintenance intent is stated directly, and filesystem changes are part of the documented behavior. The risk remains moderate because the content also mentions backups and preserving in-progress tasks.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptographic-algorithm and entropy alerts point to front matter and Japanese prose, including references to Markdown files. No evidence found of cryptographic code, encoded payloads, binary blobs, or malware-like obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows YAML front matter and natural-language descriptions. The entropy signal is explained by multilingual text rather than executable or encoded content.

low_findings.title: Device File Access Alert Is Benign stderr Redirection
low_findings.description: The filesystem alert on /dev/null appears in a date parsing fallback that redirects errors. It does not read sensitive device files or persist data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line uses 2>/dev/null only to suppress date parsing errors. This is a common shell pattern and not suspicious by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes shell command substitutions and file-inspection commands such as grep, awk, wc, date, and mkdir. These patterns are not malicious here, but they require user-visible review before modifying files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The static command findings are real shell patterns in Markdown code blocks. The surrounding context supports maintenance use rather than command injection or exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Cleanup Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes moving completed tasks, splitting session logs, and creating archive folders. These behaviors can change repository files and should be run only in the intended project directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly target project maintenance files and archives. The risk is operational rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Project planning files and session logs can grow until they are hard to review. This skill checks common maintenance files and recommends cleanup, archiving, or splitting actions.
actual_capabilities: Checks Plans.md size and completed task counts against configured thresholds.
Identifies completed tasks that may be old enough to archive.
Reviews session-log.md size and suggests monthly archive splitting.
Warns when CLAUDE.md is large and suggests moving nonessential content.
Supports project-specific cleanup thresholds through a YAML configuration file.
Provides status messages for clean, cleanup-needed, and cleanup-complete cases.
limitations: It relies on Bash-oriented examples and may need adjustment on different shells.
It does not include a packaged standalone cleanup executable.
It should not move in-progress tasks without explicit review.
It does not perform network backup or external storage synchronization.

use_cases.title: Archive completed planning tasks
use_cases.description: Move older completed Plans.md items into an archive while keeping active work visible.

use_cases.title: Control session log growth
use_cases.description: Review session-log.md size and split older entries into monthly archive files.

use_cases.title: Keep project guidance concise
use_cases.description: Detect when CLAUDE.md is too large and recommend moving detailed notes into separate documents.

prompt_templates.title: Check cleanup status
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my project maintenance files and tell me whether cleanup is needed. Do not modify files yet.

prompt_templates.title: Preview archive candidates
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify old completed work before moving it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find completed Plans.md tasks that look ready for archive. Show the proposed moves and wait for approval.

prompt_templates.title: Organize session logs
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare log rotation for a large session file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check .claude/memory/session-log.md and propose a monthly archive plan for old entries.

prompt_templates.title: Run a controlled cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance with confirmation and backup checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform project cleanup after showing a dry run, creating backups, and preserving all in-progress tasks.

output: Plans.md is over the configured line limit.
Eight completed tasks appear old enough to archive.
Recommended action: preview the archive move before editing.

output_examples.input: Review my session log for maintenance.
output: The session log is above the size threshold.
Older entries can be split into monthly archive files.
No changes were made because approval is required.

output_examples.output: CLAUDE.md is large. Move long reference sections into docs and keep only always-needed instructions in the main file.

best_practices: Run a dry review before allowing file edits or archive moves.
Keep backups of planning and session files before cleanup.
Confirm that in-progress tasks are excluded from archive actions.
anti_patterns: Do not run cleanup across unrelated directories or repositories.
Do not archive tasks only because a file is long.
Do not treat generated recommendations as a substitute for human approval.

faq.question: Does this skill delete files?
faq.answer: The documented workflow focuses on moving and archiving project content. Review proposed changes before allowing edits.

faq.question: Which files does it inspect?
faq.answer: It targets Plans.md, .claude/memory/session-log.md, CLAUDE.md, and an optional cleanup configuration file.

faq.question: Can thresholds be customized?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation describes cleanup settings in .claude-code-harness.config.yaml.

faq.question: Is it safe for active tasks?
faq.answer: It says in-progress tasks should not be moved, but users should still review archive candidates.

faq.question: Does it use the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network requests or external service calls in the audited files.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses Bash and file maintenance actions. That is useful, but it can change local project files.
